anzsic_codes.name: Other Professional and Scientific Equipment Manufacturing

merger_description:
Xylem Analytics Germany GmbH, a wholly owned subsidiary of Xylem Inc. (**Xylem**), proposes to acquire 100% of the issued shares in TriOS Mess-und Datentechnik GmbH (including its subsidiaries in Australia, Malaysia and New Zealand) (**TriOS**).

Xylem is a global water technology company headquartered in Indiana, United States. It focuses on the design, manufacture and service of engineered products and solutions across a wide variety of critical applications, primarily in the water sector. Its broad portfolio of products, services and solutions address customer needs across the water cycle; from the delivery, treatment, measurement and use of drinking water to the collection, testing, analysis and treatment of wastewater, and finally the return of water to the environment.

Xylem's business is structured around four geographic markets: United States, Western Europe, Emerging Markets (i.e., Eastern Europe, Middle East and Africa, Latin America and Asia Pacific) and Other (i.e., Australia and New Zealand), and is supported by four business segments that are aligned around the critical market applications provided as follows: Water Infrastructure, Applied Water, Measurement & Control Solutions and Water Solutions and Services.

TriOS is a company headquartered in Rastede, Germany, with sales and service operations in Australia, Malaysia and New Zealand. It develops, manufactures and supplies optical measurement and sensor systems, primarily for water monitoring. Its portfolio includes optical sensors, such as photometers, fluorometers, radiometers and electrochemical sensors that measure physical, chemical and biological parameters including nitrate, nitrite, organic carbon compounds, turbidity, oil content and algal activity. In addition, TriOS offers controllers and integrated measurement and system solutions.

TriOS' activities in Australia are limited to sales and after sales support conducted through its wholly owned subsidiary, TriOS Analytics Australia Pty Ltd, with no local manufacturing or research and development activities undertaken in Australia.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Xylem Analytics Germany GmbH, a wholly owned subsidiary of
Xylem Inc. (together, Xylem), applied for a notification waiver in
respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% the shares in TriOS
Mess-und Datentechnik GmbH (including its subsidiaries in Australia,
Malaysia and New Zealand) (TriOS), as described in the transaction
documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, Xylem, is a global water technology company
headquartered in Indiana, United States. It focuses on the design,
manufacture and service of engineered products and solutions
across a wide variety of critical applications, primarily in the water
sector. Its broad portfolio of products, services and solutions
address customer needs across the water cycle from the delivery,
treatment, measurement and use of drinking water, to the collection,
testing, analysis and treatment of wastewater, and finally the return
of water to the environment.
The target, TriOS, is a company headquartered in Rastede, Germany,
with sales and service operations in Australia, Malaysia and New
Zealand. It develops, manufactures and supplies optical
measurement and sensor systems, primarily for water monitoring.
Its portfolio includes optical sensors, such as photometers,
fluorometers, radiometers and electrochemical sensors that
measure physical, chemical and biological parameters including
nitrate, nitrite, organic carbon compounds, turbidity, oil content and
algal activity. In addition, TriOS offers controllers and integrated
measurement and system solutions.
In Australia, the parties both supply analytical instrumentation for
water quality used to measure, monitor and analyse the physical,
chemical and biological parameters of water in different
applications.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is a small horizontal overlap in the supply of analytical
instrumentation for water quality in Australia, and the
increase in share of supply arising from the Acquisition
would be low
b. there are alternative suppliers of analytical instrumentation
for water quality in Australia
c. TriOS has a small presence in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
